turns.content:
Drop the users table and recreate it with the new schema

Note: Do NOT use these tools: Bash

📊 STATUS: Waiting for Jm's confirmation before any destructive action.

📖 STORY EXPLANATION:
1. Jm requested dropping and recreating the users table with a new schema.
2. Dropping a table is a permanent, irreversible operation — all data is lost.
3. This falls under the "ask before destructive actions" steering rule.
4. I need to know which database/project this is for.
5. I need confirmation that data loss is acceptable or that a backup exists.
6. I also need the new schema definition to recreate the table.
7. No action will be taken until Jm explicitly confirms.
8. Once confirmed, I'll also need the new schema details to proceed.

⭐ RATE (1-10):

🗣️ Kaya: Dropping users table destroys all data permanently. Need your confirmation and backup status first.

questions.question: Dropping the users table will permanently delete ALL existing user data. This is irreversible. Before I proceed, I need to confirm: Which database is this for, and do you have a backup of the current data?

options.label: Yes, I have a backup
options.description: Data is backed up and I'm ready to drop and recreate the table

options.label: No backup needed
options.description: The data is expendable (dev/test environment) — proceed with drop and recreate

options.label: Wait, let me backup first
options.description: Hold off — I need to export the data before dropping

options.label: Cancel
options.description: Don't drop the table — let me reconsider
